title: Accommodation, Recreational, and Sanitation Facilities, Food and
content: 47 Catering in Ocean-Going Ships. 48 49 Accommodation and Recreational Facilities. Unless otherwise exempted herein or by 50 appropriate regulations, all ocean-going ships shall provide and maintain safe, decent, 51 and adequate accommodations, including but not limited to recreational facilities, for 52 overseas seafarers that meet the minimum standards set out in MLC 2006, as amended, 53 taking into account the need to protect the health and well-being of seafarers working or 54 living on board the ship, or both. There shall be a basic shipboard facility for women, such 55 as separate sleeping rooms. 56 57 Requirements for Sanitation. All ocean-going ships shall have sanitary facilities that 58 meet the minimum standards for health and hygiene, which shall be accessible to 59 overseas seafarers on board, and sanitation standards set by Chapter XV - "Port, Airport, 60 Vessel and Aircraft Sanitation" of P.D. No. 856, otherwise known as the "Code on 61 29 Sanitation of the Philippines", and its IRR. The sanitary facilities shall be located in 1 convenient places, allowing easy access to work stations. 2 3 Separate sanitary facilities shall also be provided for male and female crew members of 4 the ship. 5 6 Food and Catering. All ocean-going ships shall ensure the protection and promotion of 7 the health of the overseas seafarers. Shipowners shall ensure that their ocean-going 8 ships carry on board and serve food and drinking water of appropriate quality, nutritional 9 value, and quantity that adequately cover the requirements of the ship and take into 10 consideration the differing cultural and religious backgrounds of the seafarers on board 11 the ship. Seafarers on board a ship shall be provided with food free of charge during the 12 period of engagement. The food, drinks, and the preparation thereof shall be in 13 accordance with the standards of P.D. No. 856, pertinent laws, rules, regulations, and 14 international standards. In all cases, the ship's cooks shall be trained and qualified for 15 their position. 16 17
